reforms.description: The government has launched consultations to facilitate class-action lawsuits applicable to damages from anti-competitive practices, which should pave the way for a new law in 2013.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While facilitating class-action lawsuits can enhance competition and consumer protection, the impact on innovation is uncertain as it may deter some business practices.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has launched consultations on this matter, which should pave the way for a new law in 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses legal frameworks affecting competition, which can indirectly influence innovation dynamics.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched consultations on this matter, which should pave the way for a new law in 2013.

reforms.description: The French government is reforming the labour market to provide employees with new rights and employers with greater flexibility, including the possibility to negotiate firm-level agreements. Additionally, the 2012 National Pact for Growth, Competitiveness and Employment distinguishes internationally oriented strategic clusters from those dedicated to regional development, evaluating projects based on their economic outcomes and innovation diffusion.
reforms.package_name: Labour Market and Innovation Clusters Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance flexibility and innovation in the labour market, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into effect as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes in the labour market and innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The 2012 National Pact for Growth, Competitiveness and Employment includes a commitment to distinguish the internationally oriented strategic clusters from those dedicated to regional development.

reforms.description: The French government launched a programme of over 150,000 non-work-study wage-subsidised contracts aimed at improving youth employment through long-duration contracts. Additionally, there is an initiative to shift public funding for higher education towards universities to enhance their autonomy and financial resources.
reforms.package_name: Youth Employment and Higher Education Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to improve the skills and employability of youth, which is essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes have been actively rolled out as part of the government's employment strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly impact youth employment and the funding structure of higher education.
reforms.source_quote: The government has launched a new programme of over 150,000 non-work-study wage-subsidised contracts in 2013 and 2014.

reforms.description: The French government launched the 'Plan Campus' initiative with a funding of EUR 5 billion aimed at renovating campus buildings and enhancing the infrastructure of higher education institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in infrastructure is crucial for improving the quality of education and research capabilities, which can lead to enhanced innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been executed as part of the government's commitment to enhance higher education.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in the physical infrastructure of higher education.
reforms.source_quote: The Plan Campus, launched at the end of 2008 with funding of EUR 5 billion, covers the renovation of campus buildings.

reforms.description: The 'investing in the future' programme allocates EUR 35 billion over 10 years to benefit the higher education and research sector, with a significant portion directed towards 'clusters of excellence'.
reforms.package_name: Future Investment in Education and Research
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This substantial funding is aimed at enhancing research capabilities and fostering innovation through targeted investments.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively funded and implemented as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a major commitment to funding research and education.
reforms.source_quote: About two-thirds of the 2010 'investing in the future' programme, with total funding of 35 billion over 10 years, will benefit the higher education and research sector.
